stem: A carpenter builds wooden planter boxes, each in the shape of a right rectangular prism. Each box has a height of 14 inches. The width of each box's base is x inches, and the length of the base is 2 inches more than the width. Which function V gives the volume of each box, in cubic inches, in terms of the width of the box's base?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The volume of a right rectangular prism equals (base area) × (height). The base is a rectangle whose width is x inches and whose length is (x + 2) inches, so its area is x(x + 2) square inches. Multiplying by the height of 14 inches gives V(x) = 14x(x + 2).

Choice A uses (x − 2) instead of (x + 2). Choices B and C treat 14 as a separate linear dimension rather than a height multiplier.
